pattern: Greedily picking, among all currently affordable projects, the one with the highest profit and rolling its return into capital maximizes the final total after at most k picks.
hints: At each step you can only start projects whose capital requirement is met by your current funds.
Among the affordable ones, the most profitable choice is always best because profit only ever grows your future options.
Sort projects by capital, and as capital rises unlock newly affordable ones into a max-heap by profit; pop the best up to k times.

edge_inputs.description: must take lower profit first to unlock higher

stress.description: random projects with capital reachable from initial funds, random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50))
    profits = [rng.randint(0, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    capital = [rng.randint(0, 30) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    w = rng.randint(0, 10)
    return [k, w, profits, capital]
